data.title: Historic German Bund Selloff Might Not Be Prelude to Bear Trend — Market Talk
data.description: Last week's historic selloff in German Bunds, which resulted from Germany's large-scale fiscal-loosening plans, "may not prelude a bear trend," Citi Research's strategists say in a note. While the market may want to test the 3% level for the 10-year Bund, the focus may soon shift to more immediate drivers, which lean bullish, including continuing European Central Bank rate cuts, they say. Thus, tentatively, Citi strategists' base case for the 10-year Bund yield is between 2.25% and 2.75% for the quarters ahead. The 10-year Bund yield rises 2.5 basis points to last trade at 2.856%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0737 GMT - Last week's historic selloff in German Bunds, which resulted from Germany's large-scale fiscal-loosening plans, "may not prelude a bear trend," Citi Research's strategists say in a note. While the market may want to test the 3% level for the 10-year Bund, the focus may soon shift to more immediate drivers, which lean bullish, including continuing European Central Bank rate cuts, they say. Thus, tentatively, Citi strategists' base case for the 10-year Bund yield is between 2.25% and 2.75% for the quarters ahead. The 10-year Bund yield rises 2.5 basis points to last trade at 2.856%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0737 GMT - The dollar trades steady but remains at weaker levels after losses last week amid uncertainty over U.S. President Trump's policies. The big issue for markets is the fact that Trump's policies change as "often as the direction of the wind does," Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. Trump's whipsawing on tariffs makes it impossible for market participants to price risk or accurately discount a future policy path, he says. There is no surprise that "nobody wants to touch dollar denominated assets right now" with the DXY dollar index suffering its worst week in two-and-a-half years last week, he says. The DXY trades flat at 103.853 after hitting a four-month low of 103.458 Friday.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - The steepening of the German government bond curve opens up the prospect of increased demand for eurozone debt from Japanese investors, says MUFG's Derek Halpenny in a note. "The steepening of the yield curve means for Japanese investors there is a better return on hedged fixed income purchases in the euro-zone," the head of research for global markets in EMEA says. The interest-rate cut from the European Central Bank and the Bank of Japan's rate hike in January mean the cost of hedging for Japanese investors is cheapening, Halpenny says. The two- to 10-year German yield spread--which is currently 59 basis points, according to Tradeweb--widened to close to 60 basis points last week, the highest since July 2022, he says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0706 GMT - The Nikkei Stock Average ended 0.4% higher at 37028.27 as gains in auto and tech shares helped offset losses in heavy-industry and pharmaceutical stocks. Subaru gained 2.5% and SoftBank Group added 1.4%, while Mitsubishi Heavy Industries dropped 5.8% and Chugai Pharmaceutical lost 3.7%. The broader Topix index fell 0.3% to 2700.76. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield rose 5.5 basis points to 1.575%, the highest level since October 2008. Investors are focusing on U.S. trade and foreign policies. USD/JPY was at 147.69, compared with 148.03 late Friday in New York. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0654 GMT - With more issuance than redemptions and coupon payments, net issuance of U.S. Treasurys will be positive in the next five week's, Morgan Stanley Research's Matthew Hornbach calculates. Over the next five weeks, Morgan Stanley expects $460 billion of supply against $178.7 billion of redemptions and $24.2 billion of coupon payments, the strategist says in a note. This week, the U.S. Treasury will issue $58 billion in three-year notes on Tuesday, $39 billion in 10-year notes on Wednesday and $22 billion in 30-year bonds on Thursday. The 10-year Treasury yield last trades at 4.297%, down 3 basis points, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0517 GMT - Japan's real wages will likely keep falling in the near term, as the speed of price increases outpace that of wage growth, SMBC Nikko Securities economists say. Government data released Monday showed that inflation-adjusted wages fell 1.8% in January from a year earlier, the first decline in three months. "In February and March 2025, revived subsidies for electricity and gas will likely slow price increases, helping the fall in real wages shrink. But that's not enough to push them positive," the economists say. They expect real wages to turn positive in the latter half of this year when food inflation is expected to ease and this year's wage hikes go into effect.(megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: ASE Technology Reports Higher February Revenue
data.description:
ASE Technology Holding reported Monday net revenue of 44.96 billion New Taiwan dollars ($1.37 billion) in February, up 13.1% from a year earlier.

Net revenue for the month was down 9.1% from the January amount of NT$49.44 billion.

data.title: HTC Incurs NT$3.4 Billion in 2024 Loss
data.description:
HTC incurred a loss attributable to owners of the parent of NT$3.4 billion, or NT$4.11 per share, in 2024, according to a Friday Taiwan bourse filing.

The company did not provide comparative year-ago figures.

Operating revenue stood at NT$3.08 billion, the smart mobile devices manufacturer said.

data.title: JX Advanced Metals Sets IPO Price at Y820 Apiece
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Eneos Holdings' metals business plans to offer shares at 820 yen, equivalent to $5.54, apiece in its initial public offering.

This compares with the previously projected offering price of approximately Y815.

Eneos announced in February that it planned to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise billions of dollars, partly to fund investments needed to expand the supply of next-generation energy sources, such as synthetic fuels and hydrogen.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Turkish Industrial Output Growth Slows Sharply
data.description:
Industrial production in Turkey grew by 1.2% year-on-year in January 2025, moderating sharply from December's 10-month high of 7.0%.

It was the third straight month of increase but the slowest pace in the sequence, as output weakened sharply for both for manufacturing (1.2% vs 6.7% in December) and electricity, gas, and air-conditioning supply (5.0% vs 11.4%).

At the same time, mining and quarrying production fell by 0.7%, reversing a 1.7% rise in December.

On a monthly basis, industrial production declined by 2.3%, swinging from a 21-month peak of 5.0% gain in December and representing the first drop in three months.

data.title: MTQ Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
MTQ repurchased 64,300 shares for SG$16,367 on Friday, according to a same-day filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The engineering and oilfield services firm has so far bought back around 1.2 million shares, under its current share buyback mandate.

data.title: Vascon Engineers Launches Luxury Residential Project
data.description:
Engineering, procurement, and construction, and realty company Vascon Engineers has launched a luxury residential project 'Vascon Orchids' in Mumbai, India, according to a Monday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The project has a total estimated gross development value (GDV) of 3 billion rupees for its units.

Vascon Orchids will comprise three wings featuring 62 units.

Shares of the company were down over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Ferguson Enterprises' Fiscal Q2 Results Likely to Meet Expectations, Morgan Stanley Says
data.description:
Ferguson Enterprises' performance for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 is expected to be in line with consensus, as end market softness and pricing pressures keep growth subdued, Morgan Stanley said in an earnings preview.

Analysts expect the plumbing and heating products distributor's volume trends to be consistent with the first and fourth quarters of 2024, with deflation in commodity prices mostly offset by flat finished goods pricing.

"Overall, we forecast ~0% organic growth in the quarter, in-line with VA consensus of 0%. While there will be some focus on profitability given the operating margin miss at Q1, we note Q2 is seasonally Ferguson's lowest margin quarter, and forecast 7.3% margins, in-line with consensus," Morgan Stanley wrote.

For 2025, Ferguson forecasts low-single-digit growth in net sales, with adjusted operating margins of between 9% and 9.5%. The consensus estimate for revenue stands at $30.3 billion, up 2.3% year over year, with an operating profit of $2.76 billion.

Morgan Stanley has an overweight rating on the stock.

data.title: Great Portland Estates Lets London Office Space to Next plc
data.description:
Great Portland Estates leased 11,500 square feet of fully managed office space at its 31 Alfred Place property in London to Next plc .

The British retailer signed a five-year lease with the real estate company and will move into the building at the end of March, according to a Monday filing.

data.title: Kenyan shilling little changed, LSEG data shows
data.description:
Kenya's shilling was little changed on Monday, data from the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) showed.

At 0709 GMT the shilling was trading at 129.00/129.50 to the U.S. dollar, compared to Friday's close of 128.90/129.40

data.title: Vardhman Polytex to Close Operations at Ludhiana, India Manufacturing Unit; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Vardhman Polytex has decided to close operations at its manufacturing unit in Ludhiana, India, according to a Monday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company decided to close to operations to explore various avenues for the monetization of real estate potential of the land. The closure is expected to be completed by March 31.

This sale is part of the company's plan to raise funds to repay outstanding liabilities to lenders and support the company's growth initiatives.

Shares of the company were up 5% in recent trade.

data.title: SITC International Holdings Logs Higher Profit for 2024; EPS, Revenue Exceed Estimates; Shares Up 8%
data.description:
SITC International Holdings' attributable profit rose to $1.03 billion in 2024 from $531.4 million in 2023, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Earnings per share at the marine shipping services company increased to $0.38 from $0.20 a year earlier. Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected EPS of $0.35.

Revenue for the year grew 25.9% to $3.06 billion from $2.43 billion a year earlier. The Visible Alpha consensus estimate was $3.01 billion.

The company attributed the increase in revenue to increases in container shipping volume and average freight rate.

The company declared a final dividend of HK$1.40 per share, payable May 22 to shareholders on record as of May 12.

Shares of the company jumped nearly 8% in recent trade.

data.title: Volkswagen's Traton sees tepid truck market in 2025
data.description:
Volkswagen's truck unit Traton guided for a tepid commercial vehicle market in 2025 on Monday after persistent weakness in Europe, particularly in Germany, weighed on last year's results.

"We expect the global economy to lose some momentum in 2025," Traton said in a statement, adding that it would continue to focus on efficiency measures this year.

The truck maker forecast 2025 sales development in a range of -5% to +5% and an operating return on sales of between 7.5% and 8.5%. The outlook is subject to geopolitical developments, particularly in the United States, it said.

The Scania owner's shares were up 1.3% in early Frankfurt trade.

European truck makers' shares have risen significantly this year on hopes of higher order books ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs, decisions to move more production to the Unites States and an improving European outlook.

Sweden's Volvo in January reported strong order intake for the fourth quarter of 2024, and some analysts said this might be a sign of brightening outlook for the European truck market.

Traton's adjusted operating return on sales, its main profitability metric, rose to 9.2% in 2024, exceeding both the 2023 figure and its forecast, although that was mainly thanks to efficiency measures while vehicle sales fell.

Annual sales and adjusted operating result were stable at 47.47 billion euros ($51.47 billion) and 4.38 billion euros, respectively.

Sales of electric vehicles (EV) dropped by 17% to 1,739 units sold in 2024, reducing EVs' share in overall sales from 0.6% to 0.5%. However, incoming EV orders jumped by 60% to about 4,000 trucks and vans, Traton said.

Waning EV sales increase the risk of Traton not meeting carbon emission reduction targets, it added.

European truck makers struggled through last year amid falling sales, after pent-up demand following the pandemic drove them to record highs in 2023.

Traton's results come as its parent Volkswagen undergoes a major restructuring with thousands of job cuts in Germany due to shrinking European demand, rising competition from China and uncertainties around the EV transition.

($1 = 0.9223 euros)

data.title: Longfor Group Holdings Flags Up to 40% Lower Profit for 2024
data.description:
Longfor Group Holdings expects a 35% to 40% decrease in its core net attributable profit for 2024 compared with 11.35 billion yuan from 2023, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The residential property manager company attributed the expected decrease to a drop in recognized revenue and gross profit margins from the company's property development segment, resulting from the downturn in the real estate industry.

Shares of the company were down nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Tai Twun Enterprise Decides Against Paying Dividend for 2024
data.description:
Tai Twun Enterprise will not distribute dividends for 2024 following a board resolution on Friday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

The electronic connector manufacturer did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: Alliance Pharma Valued at $468 Million Under Increased Offer From Aegros Bidco
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

Alliance Pharma said it has accepted an increased cash offer from Aegros Bidco, which values the London-listed company at approximately 362 million pounds ($467.8 million).

The medicine and healthcare-product supplier said Monday that the latest offer would see each Alliance Pharma shareholder receiving 64.75 pence per share in cash.

This represents a 3.6 per cent premium over the original cash offer price of 62.5 pence in cash per Alliance share, it said. Shares last traded at 63 pence.

Alliance's directors said they find the terms of the final offer to be fair and reasonable and recommended unanimously that the company's shareholders vote in favor of the offer on March 13.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: RHB Bank's Three-Year Strategy Looks Ambitious But Achievable — Market Talk
data.description: RHB Bank's newly unveiled three-year strategy seems ambitious but achievable, given its strong performance in 2024, MIDF Research analyst Imran Yassin Yusof says in a note. The lender targets ROE of over 12% by 2027, a cost-to-income ratio below 44.8% and a gross impaired loans ratio under 1.3%, he notes. However, achieving 7% domestic loan growth and 10% fee-based income growth may be challenging, depending on market conditions, he reckons. Any slowdown in domestic growth could pressure loan expansion, while weak financial markets may impact wealth management growth, he adds. MIDF maintains a buy rating on RHB and keeps its target price at MYR7.40. Shares are 0.3% higher at MYR6.90. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)
